title: teucrium polium subsp. aragonense
intro: Teucrium polium subsp. aragonense, also known as Aragonese germander, is a subspecies of the Germander plant, which belongs to the Lamiaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Teucrium polium subsp. aragonense include its ability to reduce oxidative stress, improve cognitive function, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
